note: Legit 6-9 three-level scorer with advanced footwork for creation and real competitiveness — the rare scoring prospect profile. My concern is consistency: too many nights he coasts until the second half, and he still has to prove he can impact winning for 40 minutes, not just take over quarters.
source: Adapted from ESPN Draft Confidential (scouts/coaches on the No. 1 pick race) and Floor & Ceiling scouting notes

traits.evidence: Legit 6-9 three-level scorer
advanced footwork for creation

note: Dynamic 6-6 shotmaker who showed a level of shot-making at Kansas nobody knew existed — gets to the rim, scores at all levels, and keeps improving as a distributor. Not a complete point guard yet; he may always need a second handler next to him. Medical flag: the durability issues from last season need to be behind him.
source: Adapted from ESPN Draft Confidential and Floor & Ceiling scouting notes

traits.evidence: Dynamic shotmaker who showed a level of shot-making at Kansas nobody knew existed — scores at all levels
gets to the rim, scores at all levels
keeps improving as a distributor
Not a complete point guard yet; he may always need a second handler next to him

note: Highest floor in the draft and the ceiling is underrated. Started his career as an at-rim bully and has grown into a face-up threat who pushes in transition and creates his own shot from the perimeter. The knock from front offices is athletic limitation — no run-and-jump pop, and there's a real question whether he guards 4s or 5s at the next level.
source: Adapted from ESPN Draft Confidential and Floor & Ceiling scouting notes

note: Explosive athlete with a go-go-go motor and a 7-foot wingspan who thrived at the rim and in transition all season. The question is what happens when he isn't dunking — can he finish through contact with touch, and can he create anything rather than just finishing plays? A bit one-note offensively at UNC.
source: Adapted from CBS Sports 2026 draft grades and Bleacher Report (Wasserman) profiles

traits.evidence: Explosive athlete with a go-go-go motor and a 7-foot wingspan who thrived at the rim and in transition all season
go-go-go motor

can he create anything rather than just finishing plays? A bit one-note offensively at UNC

note: Combo-guard scoring punch — broke the ACC freshman scoring record with a 45-point, ten-three night, and his half-court finishing (nearly 69% at the rim) answered his biggest pre-season question. But he needs serious strength work, and the back issues are scary: eight games missed midseason and it resurfaced to end his year. Assist numbers undersell his passing because of how Louisville staggered his minutes.
source: Adapted from CBS Sports 2026 draft grades and Bleacher Report (Wasserman) profiles

note: Great overlap of positional size, fluidity, and touch — soft natural hands, a high release point, and a terrific left hand. From mid-January on he scored like a lottery pick and hit 39% from three. He badly needs strength: plays upright without much bend, and physicality bothers him on both ends.
source: Adapted from CBS Sports 2026 draft grades and Bleacher Report (Wasserman) profiles

traits.evidence: scored like a lottery pick and hit 39% from three
soft natural hands, a high release point, and a terrific left hand

physicality bothers him on both ends

note: True point guard with an elite feel for the game — one of the smartest players in the draft. Big-time shooter, ultra-reliable, graded out excellent as a pick-and-roll handler (91st percentile) and finishing at the rim (90th). The tools are the problem: solid size but no elite burst, limited rim pressure and separation against NBA athletes, and real questions about who he guards at the point of attack.
source: Adapted from Babcock Hoops draft profiles and CBS Sports 2026 draft grades

note: Productive lead guard who ran a high-major offense with poise beyond his class. Skeptics point to the level of team success and his slight frame; believers see shotmaking volume and real pull-up range. Undersized, so the margin for error defensively is thin.
source: Composite of public draft coverage (consensus board context); statistical profile per Barttorvik
